summary.intent: Rebase PR #2587 (PyGithub 2.x upgrade + GitHub App authentication) onto the latest main branch to resolve conflicts.
summary.outcome: Successfully rebased with 2 conflicts resolved, then discovered and fixed a GITHUB_TOKEN fallback bug in pyartcd/git.py where local git operations were unnecessarily calling get_github_git_auth_env() without a URL.

repo: art-tools uses 'prod' remote for upstream openshift-eng/art-tools and 'origin' for user forks
GitHub App has 4 installations across orgs: operator-framework, openshift-priv, openshift, openshift-eng
Main branch added slack channel routing to #art-release-layered-operators in build_fbc.py via PR #2662

code.finding: add_all() was calling _git_env() without URL parameter, triggering ambiguous installation detection and GITHUB_TOKEN fallback for local-only 'git add' operations
commit_push() local operations (git status, git commit) were using _git_env() without URL; only git push needs remote auth
get_github_git_auth_env(url=None) with multiple installations cannot auto-detect org and falls back to GITHUB_TOKEN PAT instead of failing fast
git submodule update --init can fetch remote repos and needs authentication, but git remote/checkout/clean/diff/add operations are purely local and don't

workflow: Jenkins MCP server can fetch and search build console logs for debugging authentication issues
Integration tests verified 3 auth code paths: PyGithub REST client via get_github_client_for_org(), GIT_ASKPASS for git CLI, and URL normalization
Ruff linting and unit tests should be run after code changes before pushing

friction: Merge conflicts required manual resolution in build_fbc.py (slack channel routing) and build_microshift.py (client initialization)
GITHUB_TOKEN fallback was silently used instead of failing, requiring Jenkins log analysis to detect
Had to trace through multiple layers (gen-assembly job → GitRepository class → get_github_git_auth_env) to find root cause
open_items: Need to verify fix by running gen-assembly pipeline again to confirm no GITHUB_TOKEN fallback occurs
Consider whether GIT_PASSWORD token logging redaction (mentioned in integration test notes) is tracked in a separate issue
May want to add explicit test coverage for local git operations using _local_env() without authentication
